key_evidence: Publication reports 10 enrolled patients and no objective responses; CTGov posts stable disease/progression categories under an ORR-like endpoint.
interpretation: Atlas 30 is not supported as CR/PR ORR; it aligns with a posted stable-disease count rather than objective response.

key_evidence: Publication reports 39 efficacy-evaluable patients with one confirmed partial response.
interpretation: Literature supports the CTGov 2.6 percent ORR, not the Atlas 6.66667 value.

key_evidence: Publication reports best overall response rate of 13 percent in 23 evaluable patients.
interpretation: Atlas 33.3333 appears tied to a histology/subtype differential endpoint rather than the overall response-rate endpoint.

key_evidence: Publication reports 10 treated patients and no objective responses.
interpretation: Literature supports the CTGov 0 percent response result; Atlas 100 appears to use the non-responder category.

key_evidence: Publication primary endpoint was tumor burden reduction rate; it reports 30 percent TBRR and one unconfirmed partial response.
interpretation: Atlas 30 is confirmed in literature as TBRR, not standard confirmed CR/PR ORR.

key_evidence: Publication reports no complete or partial treatment responses and seven stable-disease patients at first restaging.
interpretation: Atlas 24.1379 corresponds to stable-disease support in the posted CTGov endpoint, not CR/PR ORR.

literature_reported_rate_type: overall response rate defined as stable disease or better at 90 days
key_evidence: Atlas PMID reports 88 percent as stable disease or better in an earlier avasopasem plus SBRT trial; GRECO-2 NCT04698915 appears as an underway validation trial/design paper.
interpretation: Atlas 88 is literature-traceable but not a result for NCT04698915 and is not strict CR/PR ORR.

key_evidence: ClinicalTrials.gov lists no linked publications; targeted web/PubMed searches did not find a result manuscript for NCT00454194.
interpretation: No literature replacement found; CTGov posted result remains 9.8 percent partial response for the pemetrexed-only arm.

literature_reported_rate_type: CTGov RECIST objective response at 12 weeks
key_evidence: ClinicalTrials.gov lists no linked publications; CTGov has a RECIST objective response endpoint with 8.3 percent for this arm at 12 weeks.
interpretation: Atlas endpoint title is tumor progression rate, but the same numeric value can be reconciled to CTGov objective response at 12 weeks.

literature_reported_rate_type: CTGov RECIST objective response at 12 weeks
key_evidence: ClinicalTrials.gov lists no linked publications; CTGov objective response endpoint reports 0 percent for this arm at measured time points.
interpretation: Atlas 50 matches the 12-week tumor-progression-rate endpoint, not objective response.

key_evidence: Publication reports five response-evaluable patients, with two stable disease and three progressive disease as best response; no objective responses.
interpretation: Atlas 60 matches neither CR nor PR ORR and appears to reflect a non-response category in the posted CTGov ORR outcome.
